channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Job cuts at federal soil conservation agency has farmers worried
item.description: Farmers are fighting cuts to a federal agency that's been helping them improve their soil since the 1930s.
item.content:encoded: Farmers are fighting cuts to a federal agency that's been helping them improve their soil since the 1930s.
item.dc:creator: Eva Tesfaye

item.title: The battle over who should run the United States' 'Food for Peace' program
item.description: After DOGE demolished US international food aid, farm state lawmakers resurrected Food for Peace under USDA. But hunger specialists say USDA is undermining the program's humanitarian mission.
item.content:encoded: After DOGE demolished US international food aid, farm state lawmakers resurrected Food for Peace under USDA. But hunger specialists say USDA is undermining the program's humanitarian mission.
item.dc:creator: Frank Morris

item.title: Data Centers on the fast track have officials racing to catch up and offer regulations
item.description: Rising concerns over water, energy and noise have state and local lawmakers rushing to catch up as they offer proposals to regulate date centers.
item.content:encoded: Rising concerns over water, energy and noise have state and local lawmakers rushing to catch up as they offer proposals to regulate date centers.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Bottar

item.title: Every year hundreds of teenagers come together to judge dirt
item.description: A national competition in Oklahoma draws hundreds of teenagers so they can show off their skill judging soil — something that's important for growing crops, building houses and land management.
item.content:encoded: A national competition in Oklahoma draws hundreds of teenagers so they can show off their skill judging soil — something that's important for growing crops, building houses and land management.
item.dc:creator: Anna Pope

item.title: Reigning champion Argentina escapes with remarkable World Cup victory over Egypt
item.description: Egypt was leading late, up 2-0. The Argentinians looked beaten. But they fought and fought and fought. Scoring one goal, then another to equalize. And, finally, a third to advance to the quarterfinal.
item.content:encoded:
Egypt was leading late, up 2-0. The Argentinians looked beaten. But they fought and fought and fought. Scoring one goal, then another to equalize. And, finally, a third to advance to the quarterfinal.

(Image credit: Elsa)
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd

item.title: As climate change damages streets and highways, the road ahead may be expensive
item.description: Heat waves are becoming more common and intense as a result of climate change — and roads are suffering as a result. Are the nation's roads up to meeting the challenge of a warmer, wetter future?
item.content:encoded:
Heat waves are becoming more common and intense as a result of climate change — and roads are suffering as a result. Are the nation's roads up to meeting the challenge of a warmer, wetter future?

(Image credit: Mark Felix)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Heirs of 'odious' 169-year-old Supreme Court ruling see modern parallels
item.description: Descendants of Dred Scott and Chief Justice Roger Taney spoke about reconciliation at a church in the shadow of the Supreme Court last week as the high court wrestled with race and who can be an American.
item.content:encoded:
Descendants of Dred Scott and Chief Justice Roger Taney spoke about reconciliation at a church in the shadow of the Supreme Court last week as the high court wrestled with race and who can be an American.

(Image credit: Carrie Johnson)
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: How a tiny fleck of gold changed American history
item.description: The Gold Rush lured hundreds of thousands of people to California and changed American history. It all started with a tiny gold flake.
item.content:encoded: The Gold Rush lured hundreds of thousands of people to California and changed American history. It all started with a tiny gold flake.
item.dc:creator: Clare Lombardo

item.title: Commemorative passports featuring Trump draw both delight and ire
item.description: President Trump's image on a limited-edition U.S. passport is drawing long lines from supporters -- and sharp criticism from detractors.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's image on a limited-edition U.S. passport is drawing long lines from supporters -- and sharp criticism from detractors.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: Trump administration accuses Smithsonian museum of 'extreme political activism'
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with American Historical Association's Sarah Weicksel about the Trump administration's report accusing a Smithsonian museum of "extreme political activism."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with American Historical Association's Sarah Weicksel about the Trump administration's report accusing a Smithsonian museum of "extreme political activism."
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
